top_signals.title: U.S. death toll rises again as Iran war escalates over the weekend
top_signals.summary: Centcom says it hit Iran's coastal surveillance and air defense facilities a day after two U.S. troops were killed in an Iranian strike on Jordan.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Boeing CEO says company still needs 'a couple more years' to repair finances before new jet
top_signals.summary: CEO Kelly Ortberg signalled Boeing is focused more on stabilizing its existing business than rushing to develop a successor to its best-selling 737 MAX.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Morgan Stanley cashes in on AI boom with debt deals
top_signals.summary: Wall Street bank has emerged as chief architect of financing structures behind build-out of data centres
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: How the UK can be cleverer about stamp duty
top_signals.summary: Complaints about stamp duty are as old as the tax
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: India’s ‘Cockroach’ protests mount on streets of Delhi
top_signals.summary: Indian capital sees biggest demonstration in recent years as anger over education system mounts

top_signals.title: Rossella: The family business defying the hospitality crisis
top_signals.summary: The UK hospitality industry is grappling with an unprecedented crisis. Rising wages, a lack of staff and surging energy bills mean that an estimated 30 per cent of operators are now loss-making, according to UK Hospitality. As restaurants and bars close their doors, it’s estimated that 200 jobs a day are disappearing from the sector.  [...]
